name: reset_synchronizer
description: A reset synchronizer primitive with asynchronous assertion and synchronous deassertion in the destination clock domain.

source_refs.notes: Concrete reset synchronizer primitive with configurable synchronizer depth.

parameters.name: STAGES
parameters.description: Number of flip-flop stages. Must be >= 2 for normal CDC use.

parameters.name: RESET_ACTIVE_LEVEL
parameters.description: Active level of rst_in and rst_out. 1 means active-high reset, 0 means active-low reset.

ports.name: rst_out
ports.description: Reset synchronized for release in the clk domain. It uses the same active polarity as rst_in.

behavior: When rst_in is active, rst_out becomes active immediately or through an asynchronous reset path.
When rst_in becomes inactive, the inactive value shifts through STAGES flip-flops.
rst_out deasserts only on a rising edge of clk after STAGES cycles.
constraints: Use asynchronous assertion and synchronous deassertion.
Use at least two synchronizer stages unless the design has a documented reason.
Instantiate one reset synchronizer per destination clock domain.
Do not use this primitive to synchronize arbitrary data buses.
Do not use this primitive as a general pulse synchronizer.
implementation_notes: Parameterize polarity so rst_in and rst_out are both active-high when RESET_ACTIVE_LEVEL=1 and both active-low when RESET_ACTIVE_LEVEL=0.
Use a shift register initialized to the active reset value on assertion.
On each clk edge after deassertion, shift in the inactive reset value until rst_out releases.
Apply async_reg or equivalent synthesis attributes to the synchronizer stages when supported by the flow.
test_strategy: Assert reset between clock edges and confirm rst_out asserts promptly.
Deassert reset between clock edges and confirm rst_out releases only on clock edges.
Check release latency equals STAGES clock cycles.
Test both active-high and active-low reset configurations if parameterized.
verification_goals: rst_out is active whenever rst_in is active.
rst_out never deasserts except on clk rising edge.
